question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request for a block containing more than maxTransactionsInBlock transactions, causing all overflow transactions to be silently demoted to the otherTransactions list (hash-only), so that any Rosetta client that does not follow up with /block/transaction calls never sees those transactions' full details, effectively suppressing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed ordering of block.Transactions[0:s.maxTransactionsInBlock] to ensure that a specific target transaction is always placed beyond the cap into otherTransactions, by flooding the block with enough earlier transactions so the target is never included in the primary response, preventing it from being gossiped to clients that ignore otherTransactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier that resolves to a block whose ConsensusStartNanos equals ConsensusEndNanos, causing FindBetween() to return zero transactions and the block response to contain an empty transaction set, preventing any transaction from being gossiped for that block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /block/transaction request with a TransactionIdentifier.Hash that is a valid hex string but does not match any transaction in the block's consensus range, causing FindByHashInBlock() to return ErrTransactionNotFound and the transaction to be permanently inaccessible via this endpoint, suppressing its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call /block for the same block index, causing the entityCache LRU to be filled with attacker-controlled account alias mappings via cache poisoning through GetAccountAlias(), so that subsequent legitimate clients receive wrong account identifiers in transaction operations, corrupting gossiped transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a cache miss storm by requesting blocks with many unique account IDs, exhausting the entityCache LRU capacity (MaxSize), causing repeated GetAccountAlias() database calls that degrade performance to the point where transaction gossip via the /block endpoint is effectively denied?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a nil BlockIdentifier, causing RetrieveBlock() to call RetrieveLatest() and return the second-latest block, so that the most recent block's transactions are never returned and are effectively hidden from gossip consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block/transaction request where BlockIdentifier.Hash contains only the '0x' prefix (empty after SafeRemoveHexPrefix()), causing FindByIdentifier() to receive an empty hash string and return ErrInvalidArgument, preventing the transaction from being fetched and gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent /block requests for a block with many transactions, causing FindBetween() to execute many parallel database queries that exhaust connection pool resources, resulting in a denial-of-service that prevents transaction gossip for all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that otherTransactions only contains TransactionIdentifier.Hash values (no operation data) to cause a Rosetta client to miss the full transaction details of overflow transactions, since the client must make a separate /block/transaction call that may fail or be rate-limited, effectively preventing those transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a very large block index (e.g., int64 max value) to FindByIndex(), causing an unbounded database query or integer overflow that results in an error response, preventing any transaction data from being returned and gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block/transaction request with a BlockIdentifier.Index that does not match the block found by BlockIdentifier.Hash, causing FindByIdentifier() to return ErrBlockNotFound and preventing the transaction from being fetched, effectively suppressing its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the /block endpoint to send thousands of requests per second, exhausting server goroutines or memory through concurrent FindBetween() calls, causing a sustained denial-of-service that prevents all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in the entityCache by sending concurrent /block requests that simultaneously read and write the same account ID entry, causing a stale or incorrect AccountId to be cached and returned in transaction operations, corrupting gossiped transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a hash that matches a valid block but with a mismatched index, causing RetrieveBlock() via FindByIdentifier() to return ErrBlockNotFound, preventing all transactions in that block from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that block.Transactions is sliced to block.Transactions[0:s.maxTransactionsInBlock] in-place, causing a memory aliasing issue where the underlying array still holds references to overflow transactions, potentially leaking transaction data or causing incorrect gossip behavior under concurrent access?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block/transaction request with a TransactionIdentifier.Hash that is a valid hex string of all zeros, causing FindByHashInBlock() to query for a non-existent transaction and return ErrTransactionNotFound, while consuming database resources and preventing legitimate transaction gossip requests from being served?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request for a genesis block, causing RetrieveGenesis() to be called and returning a block with ConsensusStartNanos == ConsensusEndNanos == 0, which causes FindBetween() to return no transactions, preventing genesis block transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /block request for a block whose transactions contain operations with account IDs that are not present in the account repository, causing GetAccountAlias() to return an error for every operation and the entire Block() call to fail, preventing all transactions in that block from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier containing only a hash (no index), causing RetrieveBlock() to call FindByHash() with a crafted hash that matches a different block than intended, returning wrong transactions and corrupting the gossip view of that block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxTransactionsInBlock cap by sending repeated /block requests for a block with exactly maxTransactionsInBlock+1 transactions, causing the last transaction to always appear only in otherTransactions, and if the client never calls /block/transaction for it, that transaction is never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block/transaction request with a TransactionIdentifier.Hash that has a valid hex prefix '0x' followed by an odd number of hex characters, causing SafeRemoveHexPrefix() to strip the prefix but hex.DecodeString() in FindByHashInBlock() to fail, returning ErrInvalidTransactionIdentifier and preventing the transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a negative block index, causing FindByIndex() to return ErrInvalidArgument, preventing any transactions in that block from being returned and gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LRU cache eviction policy in entityCache to cause a previously cached account alias to be evicted and re-fetched with a different value from the database, resulting in inconsistent account identifiers across different /block responses for the same transaction, corrupting the gossiped transaction view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request that causes FindBetween() to return a very large number of transactions (far exceeding maxTransactionsInBlock), causing the otherTransactions slice allocation to consume excessive memory and potentially cause an out-of-memory condition that crashes the service and prevents all transaction gossip?
